name: daytona
description: Manage Daytona dev sandboxes — list, create, inspect, and delete sandboxes and run shell commands inside them

envVars.name: DAYTONA_API_KEY
envVars.description: Daytona API key created in the Dashboard under API Keys; same env var used by the Daytona SDKs and CLI

tools.name: List Sandboxes
tools.description: List sandboxes in the organization with optional name, label, and state filtering

labels.description: JSON-encoded labels to filter by, e.g. {"env":"prod"}

states.description: Filter by sandbox states, e.g. started, stopped, archived, error

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from a previous response (nextCursor)

historicalSummary.omitted: resource limits, backup and network settings, and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: Create Sandbox
tools.description: Create a new sandbox, optionally from a snapshot and with custom resources (starts billable compute)

name.description: Sandbox name (defaults to the sandbox ID if not provided)

snapshot.description: ID or name of the snapshot to create the sandbox from (omit for the default image)

env.description: Environment variables to set inside the sandbox

target.description: Target region where the sandbox will be created, e.g. us, eu

cpu.description: CPU cores allocated to the sandbox

memory.description: Memory allocated to the sandbox in GB

disk.description: Disk space allocated to the sandbox in GB

autoStopInterval.description: Auto-stop interval in minutes (0 disables auto-stop)

public.description: Whether the sandbox HTTP preview is publicly accessible

tools.name: Get Sandbox
tools.description: Get sandbox details by ID or name, including state, snapshot, resources, and timestamps

sandboxIdOrName.description: ID or name of the sandbox

tools.name: Delete Sandbox
tools.description: Delete a sandbox by ID or name, discarding its state and filesystem

sandboxIdOrName.description: ID or name of the sandbox to delete

tools.name: Execute Command
tools.description: Execute a shell command inside a running sandbox via the toolbox and return its exit code and output (legacy REST route; Daytona's current toolbox API is SDK-first)

sandboxId.description: ID of the running sandbox to execute the command in

cwd.description: Working directory to execute the command in

tools.name: Start Sandbox
tools.description: Start a stopped sandbox — required before Execute Command, since sandboxes auto-stop after the autoStopInterval (15 minutes by default)

tools.name: Stop Sandbox
tools.description: Stop a running sandbox to pause billable compute without destroying its state

tools.name: List Snapshots
tools.description: List available snapshots (sandbox base images) — use these names or IDs in the snapshot field of Create Sandbox

name.description: Filter by partial snapshot name match

prompts.title: Sandbox overview
prompts.prompt: List my Daytona sandboxes with their state, snapshot, and resources.

prompts.title: Spin up a sandbox
prompts.prompt: Create a new Daytona sandbox and confirm it reaches the started state.

prompts.title: Run a command
prompts.prompt: Run the shell command I give you inside the Daytona sandbox I name and show me the output and exit code.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Daytona setup

steps.title: Create a Daytona account
steps.description: Sign up at https://app.daytona.io — new accounts include free usage credits, so no payment method is needed to test.

steps.title: Create an API key
steps.description: In the Daytona Dashboard go to API Keys and create a new key (it is shown only once at creation time).

steps.title: Set the environment variable
steps.description: Copy the key into your .env as DAYTONA_API_KEY.

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run List Sandboxes to confirm the key works, then create a sandbox and execute a simple command like echo hello.

notes: The API is served on https://app.daytona.io/api and authenticates with Authorization: Bearer <API key>
Sandboxes auto-stop after the autoStopInterval (default 15 minutes) unless set to 0
Execute Command requires the sandbox to be in the started state
Execute Command uses the legacy /toolbox REST route, which Daytona marks deprecated in favor of its SDKs; the richer per-sandbox toolbox API (files, git, sessions) is SDK-first and not covered here
Creating sandboxes consumes billable compute — delete sandboxes you no longer need
